summary: Install-time host reconnaissance exfiltration via curl POST
details: Package ships sec_check.js which is invoked at install time and uses child_process to execute a shell command that runs host identification (`id`) and POSTs the output to a remote endpoint via `curl -X POST`. This is a classic install-time host-reconnaissance exfiltration pattern: every developer or build system that runs `npm install` against this package has identity / host information shipped off-host to an attacker-controlled destination. Package name pattern (`lab-helper-test` under a throwaway-shaped scope) and the trivially small surface area are consistent with a test or staging release for a credential/recon collector rather than a legitimate utility.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
